behaviors.description: The embedding vector produced for a given query string has the correct dimensionality for the vector database index.
behaviors.justification: Start at Unit: a local length check is possible. Unit -> Integration proof: failure appears at the embedding-module to index-schema handoff (vector length vs configured index dimension). Integration -> System proof: this behavior can fail only with the real embedding provider/model contract (e.g., model/version returns a different dimension), which in-process mocks can hide. Rejected Agentic: behavior is dimensionality/schema compatibility, not model output quality or non-deterministic relevance.
behaviors.test_description: Run against the real embedding endpoint and real configured index schema: issue a real query, capture returned vector length, and assert exact equality with index dimension before search insert/query path.

behaviors.description: The similarity search returns results ordered by descending cosine similarity score.
behaviors.justification: Start at Unit: pure sorting logic could be unit-tested, but this behavior is about search-component output order. Unit -> Integration proof: real failure occurs at module handoff between query service and vector-store adapter (score retrieval + ordering semantics). Integration brake: no required OS/network/subprocess behavior if tested with local/in-process vector DB fixture; no LLM dependency; no multi-agentic chain. Rejected System: behavior text does not require real networked vector service or OS effects, only correct ranking semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Seed a local test vector index with known vectors, run similarity search through service + repository adapter, and assert returned items are monotonically non-increasing by cosine score.

behaviors.description: The LLM re-ranking step changes the order of at least some results compared to the raw similarity ranking when the query has nuanced intent.
behaviors.justification: Start at Unit: list-reordering code alone is insufficient. Unit -> Integration proof: failure is at handoff from retriever output to reranker input/output ordering. Integration -> System proof: requires real external reranker call path. System -> Agentic proof: correctness depends on actual LLM relevance judgments for nuanced intent; deterministic stubs can always reorder and falsely pass without validating semantic quality. Rejected Workflow: only one agentic decision step (rerank) is required, not multiple agentic stages whose combined reasoning is under test.
behaviors.test_description: Use a nuanced query and a fixed candidate set where lexical similarity differs from contextual relevance; call the real LLM reranker and assert at least one item position changes versus raw similarity order, with repeated runs/acceptance band to handle model variance.

behaviors.description: A query with no close matches in the database returns an empty result set rather than low-relevance noise.
behaviors.justification: Start at Unit: threshold predicate can be unit-tested. Unit -> Integration proof: real failure appears at boundary between similarity scores returned by search layer and API filtering/threshold policy (including top-K handling). Integration brake: no mandatory real OS/network/subprocess; no LLM output quality dependency; no multi-agentic chain. Rejected System: behavior text is about relevance-threshold behavior, which can be reproduced with a local seeded DB fixture.
behaviors.test_description: Populate local test index with intentionally distant vectors, execute query through API service stack (search + thresholding), and assert response is an empty list (and no below-threshold items leak through).

behaviors.description: The end-to-end latency from receiving the query to returning re-ranked results stays below 2 seconds for the median case.
behaviors.justification: Start at Unit: local timing of one function is insufficient. Unit -> Integration proof: latency is accumulated across embedding, retrieval, reranking, and API assembly boundaries. Integration -> System proof: requirement is end-to-end runtime and includes real external/service/transport behavior; mocked dependencies hide real network/service latency and jitter. Rejected Agentic: pass/fail is latency budget, not semantic correctness of model output quality; non-deterministic content quality is not the oracle here. Rejected Workflow: although pipeline has multiple steps, this behavior does not require validating multi-agentic reasoning outcomes, only wall-clock performance.
behaviors.test_description: Run a median-latency performance test against deployed-like stack with real embedding/vector/LLM calls over real transport, collect per-request end-to-end timings for a representative query set, and assert p50 < 2000 ms.
